questions.question: What is the minimum requirement for a database to be used as a calendar view in Notion?

options.text: At least one text property
options.explanation: Text properties are optional; the requirement is about dates.

options.text: At least one date property
options.explanation: A calendar view must contain at least one date property to anchor entries on a timeline.

options.text: At least one number property
options.explanation: Number properties are not required for a calendar view.

options.text: A property named "Event"
options.explanation: The property can be named anything; only a date type is required.

questions.question: How can you add a new entry to a Notion calendar database?

options.text: Drag an existing page onto the calendar
options.explanation: While you can move pages, the primary way described is using the plus sign.

options.text: Click the "+" sign next to a date cell
options.explanation: The article says clicking the + sign next to a date creates a new page.

options.text: Press the "Enter" key while the calendar is selected
options.explanation: There is no mention of a keyboard shortcut for adding items.

options.text: Select "New Page" from the top‑right menu
options.explanation: That adds a page to the database but not directly tied to a specific date.

questions.question: Which of the following statements about switching between different views of the same database is true?

options.text: You must create a new database for each view type you want.
options.explanation: Notion lets you switch views within the same database.

options.text: You can toggle between calendar, table, list, and board views by clicking their tabs.
options.explanation: The article explains you can switch seamlessly by clicking tabs.

options.text: Board view is only available for databases that contain a "Status" select property.
options.explanation: Board view can be used with any select or multi‑select property, not limited to "Status".

options.text: Weekly calendar view automatically hides all non‑date properties.
options.explanation: Non‑date properties remain; the view only changes the visual layout.

questions.question: If you need to track two different dates for the same entry (e.g., "Start date" and "Due date"), how does Notion support this in a calendar database?

options.text: You can add multiple date properties to the same database.
options.explanation: The article states calendars can have more than one date property for organizing entries by different dates.

options.text: You must create a second database for the additional date.
options.explanation: A single database can hold multiple date properties; no need for a second database.

options.text: Only the primary date property is shown; extra dates are ignored.
options.explanation: Extra date properties are usable; they just aren't the primary calendar anchor.

options.text: Notion only supports one date per entry; you need to use a text field for the second date.
options.explanation: Multiple date fields are explicitly supported.

questions.question: Which of the following property types cannot be added to a calendar database?

options.text: People
options.explanation: People is listed as an allowed property type.

options.text: Multi‑select menus
options.explanation: Multi‑select menus are mentioned as supported.

options.text: File uploads
options.explanation: The article lists text, numbers, selects, dates, people, etc., but does not mention file uploads as a property type.

options.text: Single‑select menus
options.explanation: Single‑select menus are explicitly allowed.

questions.question: When you click on a calendar entry, Notion lets you decide how the page opens. Which setting controls this behavior?

options.text: The "Open as" toggle in the view settings
options.explanation: The article notes you can decide how database pages open when you click them, which is configured in view settings.

options.text: The "Page layout" option in the template gallery
options.explanation: Template gallery provides whole templates, not per‑view opening behavior.

options.text: The "Default page type" in the workspace settings
options.explanation: Workspace settings affect the whole workspace, not individual view click behavior.

options.text: The "Date format" dropdown in the property settings
options.explanation: Date format changes how dates display, not how pages open.

questions.question: A team wants to see their content pipeline both as a calendar and as a kanban board without duplicating data. Based on the article, what is the best approach?

options.text: Create two separate databases, one for each view, and sync them manually.
options.explanation: Duplicating databases defeats the purpose; Notion allows multiple views on the same database.

options.text: Use a single database, add a calendar view and a board view, and switch via the view tabs.
options.explanation: The article explains you can have the same data displayed in calendar, table, list, board views by clicking tabs.

options.text: Export the calendar as CSV and import it into a board view.
options.explanation: Export/import creates separate copies and loses live synchronization.

options.text: Only Notion’s API can link a calendar to a board, so you must write custom code.
options.explanation: Built‑in view switching makes custom code unnecessary.
